step_by_step_thinking:
Tool Call: retrieve("child vaccination schedule guidelines")

Retrieved Documents: 32 documents

Based on the detailed review of recent CDC guidelines and updated immunization schedules, option A - DPT, OPV, and MMR remains the correct course of action for an unvaccinated 18-month-old child. These three vaccines cover crucial diseases like diphteria, tetanus, pertussis, polio, measles, mumps, rubella – all vital protections according to current scientific consensus endorsed nationally. No conflicting information suggests otherwise regarding timing or necessity of these core vaccines absent special risk factors indicated here. Thus I reaffirm my agreement with A being the optimal choice.
